desc: Salento is best for hikers, coffee-curious couples, and remote workers wanting a 3-4 night Cocora + finca stay, or 6-8 nights when paired with Filandia and Valle del Cauca stops.
hls: Cocora Valley Acaime loop for wax palms, hummingbirds, and cloud-forest bridges
Working coffee fincas near town with hands-on harvesting/processing tours
Walkable base around Calle Real with backpacker hostels and boutique fincas
Simple onward links to Filandia, Armenia, and Pereira for Coffee Axis itineraries

todo.name: Cocora Valley Acaime-Las Palmas loop
todo.desc: Take first Willy jeep, hike clockwise, and finish before afternoon rain builds.

todo.name: Finca El Ocaso or Las Acacias tour
todo.desc: Book an English/Spanish session covering varietals, washing, drying, and cupping.

todo.name: Alto de la Cruz sunset climb
todo.desc: Go 60-90 minutes before dusk for clearer valley views and town lights after dark.

todo.name: Calle Real craft + coffee bar crawl
todo.desc: Pair artisan shops with specialty cafés and small aguardiente bars.

todo.name: Trout lunch in Boquía
todo.desc: Ride or walk to Boquía for riverside trucha and patacones away from main-square crowds.

todo.name: Filandia + Barbas-Bremen reserve day
todo.desc: Combine Filandia viewpoint with a guided reserve walk for howler-monkey sightings.

wifi.note: Decent in town stays and cafés; finca lodges outside town can be inconsistent, especially in bad weather or on room-level coverage away from the main house.

prac.fltNote: From UK/Ireland, most routings connect via Bogotá or Medellín (sometimes Madrid/Panama City first), then a domestic leg to AXM/PEI; final transfer to Salento is by bus, shared shuttle, or private car (1-1.5h).
prac.currency: Colombian peso (COP); cash is still useful for Willy jeeps, trail snacks, smaller fincas, and some rural day-trip stops even when cards work in town.
prac.lang: Spanish is the primary language; English is most common in higher-end hotels and organized tours.
prac.tz: Colombia Time (UTC-5) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: Colombia grants visa-free short tourist stays to many nationalities, but entry officers may request onward-travel proof and sufficient-funds evidence, so confirm the current rule for your passport before relying on flexible domestic add-ons.
alerts: AXM arrivals after ~20:00 can miss the last direct bus connections—pre-book shuttle or overnight in Armenia.
Roadworks, fog, and rain-triggered slips can add 30-90 minutes on AXM/PEI-Salento transfers.
Weekend/holiday day-tripper surges create jeep queues for Cocora from early morning.
Mountain weather changes fast; carry rain layer and waterproof footwear even in drier months.

sweet: February-March and July-August usually give the best hiking value mix: decent visibility and trail access without December-January holiday price pressure.
